# init(newBigIntFrom:in:) | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ JavaScriptCore ](/documentation/javascriptcore)

- [ JSValue ](/documentation/javascriptcore/jsvalue)

- [ init(newBigIntFrom:in:) ](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-1f0xs)

- [ JSValue ](/documentation/javascriptcore/jsvalue)

-  init(newBigIntFrom:in:) 

Initializer# init(newBigIntFrom:in:)

iOS 18.0+iPadOS 18.0+Mac Catalyst 18.0+macOS 15.0+tvOS 9.0+visionOS 2.0+

```
init?(
    newBigIntFrom string: String,
    in context: JSContext
)
```

## [ Parameters ](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-1f0xs#parameters)

`string`The string representation of the BigInt JavaScript value being created.

`context`The JSContext to which the resulting JSValue belongs.

## [Return Value](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-1f0xs#return-value)

The JSValue representing a JavaScript value with type BigInt.

## [Discussion](/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue/init(newBigIntFrom:in:)-1f0xs#discussion)

Create a new BigInt value from a numeric string.

This is equivalent to calling the BigInt constructor from JavaScript with a string argument.
